formations_blocks: Uzbekistan big at the back / defensive setup
Portugal on the front foot with sustained attacking pressure
adjustments: Portugal using short corners and repeated wide service
Cancelo making forward runs to support attacks
Nuno Mendes delivering repeated dangerous crosses
Fernandes taking set pieces and involved in buildup
key_matchups: Ronaldo attacking service from wide areas
phases.summary: Portugal started brighter and immediately imposed pressure, mainly through wide progression and crossing from the flanks. Uzbekistan responded with a deep, compact defensive setup but struggled to get a foothold, and Portugal’s early momentum was rewarded when Ronaldo scored in the 7th minute.

formations_blocks: Portugal pressing high after turnovers and set pieces
Uzbekistan attempting to recover through free kicks and direct attacking responses
adjustments: Portugal repeatedly using short free-kick routines around the box
Portugal continuing to press after failed set-piece combinations
Uzbekistan becoming increasingly penalized by fouls, disrupting their defensive shape
key_matchups: Neto drawing a foul from Hamrobekov
Cancelo involved in the VAR-reviewed foul leading to the disallowed goal
Mendes winning free kicks and being central to Portugal's attacking threat
phases.summary: Portugal controlled the phase by leaning on pressure, set pieces, and quick restarts, with Mendes repeatedly at the heart of the danger. Uzbekistan’s discipline deteriorated as fouls mounted, and although they briefly responded with a goal, VAR overturned Portugal’s 29' finish, leaving the momentum still with the home side.

adjustments: Uzbekistan made changes with Alizhonov and Mozgovoy coming on
Portugal made changes with Semedo and Neto coming on for Cancelo and Conceição
phases.summary: Portugal reach half-time 3-0 ahead and are described as being in full control. The only tactical information given for the restart is that both sides have made substitutions, with Uzbekistan trying to respond and Portugal altering personnel while maintaining control.

formations_blocks: Portugal in control and Uzbekistan pushing forward
End-to-end phase with Portugal and Uzbekistan trading free kicks and corners
adjustments: Uzbekistan looked for a more aggressive push after halftime
Portugal regained control quickly after Uzbekistan set-piece moments
key_matchups: Cancelo vs an Uzbekistan attack leading to the disallowed goal
phases.summary: This phase was defined by Uzbekistan trying to force their way back into the game through shots, free kicks and corners, but they repeatedly failed to capitalize. Portugal stayed largely in control, quickly neutralizing those spells before punishing a set-piece situation for the fourth goal via an own goal. The commentary also suggests a VAR intervention had already denied Uzbekistan, keeping the momentum firmly with Portugal.

formations_blocks: Portugal in possession controlling the game
Portugal defending later phases with enough control to allow Diogo Costa to collect late set-piece delivery
adjustments: Portugal used a short-corner routine at 84'
Portugal’s late attacking move saw Ronaldo feed Mendes before a shot went wide at 90+5'
key_matchups: Rafa Leão versus Uzbekistan’s defence on the 88' finish
Cristiano Ronaldo as the intended target of a cross at 89'
Diogo Costa versus Uzbekistan’s 90+3' free kick
Ronaldo feeding Mendes in Portugal’s late attack at 90+5'
phases.summary: Portugal finished the match in control, with possession dominating the phase and a late fifth goal from Rafa Leão after a cross intended for Ronaldo deflected into his path. Uzbekistan had sporadic counter-attacks and a couple of set-piece moments, but they were wasteful and never seriously threatened the result. The only notable tactical changes were Portugal’s substitutions, which freshened the attack without altering their overall dominance.
